{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the provided documents, the mandibular central incisor is the smallest of the mandibular incisors and has a single root. The mandibular canine has a single long root and a single root canal, but it is not typically described as having greater crown bulk distal to the faciolingual dissecting plane. The mandibular lateral incisor is smaller than the canine but larger than the central incisor. The mandibular second premolar is not a mandibular tooth. Therefore, the mandibular central incisor is the most likely option to have greater crown bulk distal to the faciolingual dissecting plane.", "answer_choice": "A"}
